texto: Since we are nothing but packages of tepid, half-rotted viscera, we shall always have trouble with sentiment. Being in love is nothing, it’s sticking together that’s difficult. Faeces on the other hand make no attempt to endure or to grow. On this score we are far more unfortunate than shit: our frenzy to persist in our present state — that’s the unconscionable torture.
autor: Voyage au bout de la nuit
explicacao: Citação de Voyage au bout de la nuit sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Since we are nothing but packages of tepid, half-rotted viscera, we shall always have trouble wit…” articula motivacao, existence, existentialism em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como termino e existentialism.

texto: Just looking at her made my mouth water, like a sip of dry wine, that flinty taste. There was a hardness in her eyes, unrelieved by the amiably commercial oriental-Fragonard vivacity you find in nearly all the eyes in these parts.
autor: Voyage au bout de la nuit
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Voyage au bout de la nuit: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Just looking at her made my mouth water, like a sip of dry wine, that flinty taste. There was a h…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, love, lust pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de lust e sex.
